title: The Task Success Metric
concept: Defining binary and graded success criteria for open-ended, multi-step agent tasks.

exercises.prompt: What do we use to determine if a task is successful?
exercises.explain: We use success criteria to evaluate if a task is completed successfully.

cards.title: What is Task Success?
cards.body: Task success tells us if an agent completed a task well. It helps us understand how effective an agent is in completing challenges.

cards.title: Success Criteria
cards.body: Success criteria are rules that define whether a task is successful. They can be binary (yes/no) or graded (ranging from good to great).

cards.title: Target Practice
cards.body: Think of success criteria like aiming at a target. If you hit the bullseye, that's a clear success (binary). If you get close, like in a ring around the bullseye, that can be graded success.

cards.title: Example of Success Criteria
cards.body: For a homework assignment, a binary success criterion is: "Did the student submit the work?" A graded success might be: "Was the work very good, good, or just okay?"

cards.title: Myth: Success is Only Black or White
cards.body: Some people think success is just yes or no. But many tasks require seeing how well something is done, not just if it was done.

cards.title: Visualizing Task Success
cards.body: Here’s how success can look different:

right.text: Task done, but results varied in quality!

exercises.prompt: What type of success criteria allows for different levels of success?
exercises.explain: Graded success criteria allow for different levels of achievement, unlike binary criteria which are just yes or no.

exercises.prompt: Binary success criteria can only say if a task is completed or not.
exercises.explain: Binary criteria indicate if a task is done, but not how well it was done.

exercises.prompt: A _____ success criterion can show different levels of quality in task outcomes.
exercises.sentence: A graded success criterion can show different levels of quality in task outcomes.
exercises.explain: Graded criteria allow us to assess various levels of success.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes a graded success criterion?

options.text: It shows only if something is done or not.
It measures different levels of achievement.
It is the same as a binary success criterion.

exercises.explain: Graded success criteria provide a more detailed assessment of how well a task was accomplished.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about success criteria is true?

options.text: Graded criteria allow for more detailed evaluation.
Only binary criteria are useful in evaluating tasks.

exercises.explain: Graded criteria provide more depth in evaluating how different tasks are completed, making them very useful for assessing performance.
